Here is the complete summary of all TODO comments found across the `webapp` project:

---

## 📋 TODO Report — `webapp` Project

**2 work items found across 2 files**

---

### 1. `projects/webapp/docs/README.md` — Line 42
A high-level section listing **4 outstanding feature tasks**:

| # | Work Item |
|---|-----------|
| 1 | **Add database persistence** — the app currently uses an in-memory store only |
| 2 | **Add authentication middleware** — no auth layer exists yet |
| 3 | **Add input validation on POST** — POST requests are not validated |
| 4 | **Add pagination for user listing** — the GET endpoint returns all users at once |

---

### 2. `projects/webapp/src/UsersController.scala` — Line 20
A code-level TODO inside the `POST` branch of `UsersController`:

```scala
case "POST" =>
    // TODO: parse body, validate, add user
```

The `POST` handler is a **stub** — it needs full implementation to:
- Parse the incoming request body (JSON → User)
- Validate the parsed input
- Insert the new user into the store

---

### Summary

| Priority | File | Task |
|----------|------|------|
| 🔴 Code | `UsersController.scala:20` | Implement POST handler (parse, validate, persist) |
| 🟡 Feature | `README.md:44` | Database persistence (replace in-memory store) |
| 🟡 Feature | `README.md:45` | Authentication middleware |
| 🟡 Feature | `README.md:46` | Input validation on POST |
| 🟢 UX | `README.md:47` | Pagination for user listing |

The most immediate code gap is the unimplemented **POST handler** in `UsersController.scala`, which ties directly into the README items for persistence and validation.
